original: Discovery of 7 Earth-sized planets orbiting star Trappist-1 announced in Journal "Nature" - raises possibility of alien life
Scientists at the Roslin Institute in Scotland announce they have cloned an adult mammal, producing Dolly the sheep - born 5 July 1996 (d. 2003)
"Miracle on Ice": US ice hockey team beats heavily favoured Soviet Union, 4-3 at Lake Placid in one of the biggest upsets in Olympic history; Americans go on to win gold medal
Astronomers reveal Webb telescope data is upending existing theories of how early galaxies were formed, after finding six massive galaxies 100x larger than expected soon after the Big Bang
Odysseus is the first private spacecraft to land on the moon, albeit on its side, the first US moon landing since 1972
Neanderthals, not humans, were the first artists on Earth, producing red cave paintings 65,000 years ago in Spain, according to new research published in "Science"
Soviets launch Kosmos 110 with Veterok & Ugolek, 1st 2-dog crew
Galileo Galilei's book "Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems" is published comparing the Copernican and Ptolemaic systems and whether the Earth orbits the sun
Heinrich Hertz, German physicist, 1st to broadcast & receive radio waves, born in Hamburg, German Confederation (d. 1894)
Joseph Lechleider, American electrical engineer (invented DSL /high speed internet technology), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2015)
Francis Pharcellus Church, American editor and publisher (The New York Sun - "Yes, Virginia, There Is A Santa Claus"), born in Rochester, New York (d. 1906)
Norman Lindsay, Australian artist and novelist (The Magic Pudding), born in Creswick Victoria (d. 1969)[1]
Steve Fossett completes 1st air balloon over Pacific Ocean (9600 km)
Malcolm Campbellsets a world land speed record of 272.46 mph driving his famous Blue Bird car at Daytona Beach, Florida
Pakistani pace bowler Shoaib Akhtar bowls the fastest ball recorded in cricket history at 100.2 mph (161.3 km/h)
US wins Olympic women's ice hockey gold medal for the first time in 20 years with a 4-3 shootout victory over Canada in Pyeongchang; scores tied 3-3 after overtime
Barbara Jo Rubin becomes 1st female jockey to win at an American parimutuel race at a major US track aboard Cohesian at Charles Town, West Virginia
1st solo flight from England to Australia lands in Darwin, 15 ½ days after take off, piloted by Australian aviator Bert Hinkler
"The Little Colonel" premieres starringShirley Temple,Lionel BarrymoreandBill Robinson, featuring famous stair dance with Hollywood's first interracial dance couple
Edward Gorey, American author and artist (Curious Sofa), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2000)
Chinese short track speed skater Wu Dajing beats men's 500m world record twice en route to winning the gold medal at the Pyeongchang Winter Olympics; 2nd man in history under 40 seconds (39.584s)
NY Lotto pays $26.9 million to one winner (#s are 1-5-12-19-44-50)
Petr Svoboda scores the winner as the Czech Republic claims its first ever Olympic ice hockey gold medal with a 1-0 victory over Russia in Nagano, Japan
Pierre Janssen, French astronomer, discovered helium, born in Paris, France (d. 1907)
James Hong, American character actor (The New Adventures of Charlie Chan; Kung Fu; Kung Fu Panda), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota

(Margaret) "Marni" Nixon [McEathron], American soprano singer and film 'ghost voice' (for Audrey Hepburn in "My Fair Lady"; Natalie Wood in "West Side Story"; Deborah Kerr in "The King And I"), born in Altadena, California (d. 2016)
The Beatles begin their own music publishing company (Northern Songs)
The Beatles arrive back in England after their successful first US visit
Rodgers & Hammerstein's television musical "Cinderella", starring Lesley Ann Warren and Stuart Damon, with Pat Carroll and Celeste Holm, premieres on CBS-TV
Olave Baden-Powell, English founder of the Girl Guides, born in  Chesterfield, England (d. 1977)
Faith Thomas, Australian cricket fast bowler (1 WTest; first Indigenous woman to represent Australia in any sport; South Australia), born in Nepabunna, Australia (d. 2023)
1st successful chinchilla farm in US, in Los Angeles, California
Inaugural Daytona 500: Lee Petty and Johnny Beauchamp cross finish line side by side; Beauchamp declared unofficial winner; ruling overturned after 3 days by NASCAR founder Bill France Sr
Bruno Marie-Rose runs indoor world record 200m (20.36 sec)
Aleksandr Tikhonov of the Soviet Union earns his 4th straight gold medal as part of the 4 x 7.5k biathlon relay team at the Lake Placid Winter Olympics
Soviet biathlete Anatoly Alyabyev wins his 2nd gold medal of the Lake Placid Winter Olympics as part of the 4 x 7.5k relay team; also wins individual gold

The Beatles begin recording what becomes their "Abbey Road" album at EMI Recording Studio at 3 Abbey Road in Westminster, London, England; the studio was later renamed in honor of their achievements there
USSR launches Kosmos 57 into earth orbit (Voskhod Test)
Spaces shuttle Columbia 19 (STS 75) launches into orbit
Vladimir Salnikov (USSR) sets 1500m free style swimming record
1st English soccer match at Kunstlicht: Portsmouth vs Newcastle United
“Father of American Golf” John Reid first demonstrates golf on a Yonkers cow pasture to friends
PeterTchaikovsky's 4th Symphony in F premieres at a Russian Musical Society concert in Moscow [February 10 O.S.]
Octavius Catto, American educator (Institute for Colored Youth, 1859-71), civil rights activist, and baseball player (Philadelphia Pythians, 1865-71), born in Charleston, South Carolina (d. 1871)
James Reese Europe, American ragtime and early jazz arranger, composer, and bandleader in New York, and with the military in France during WWI, born in Mobile, Alabama (d. 1919)[1][2]
Ernie K-Doe [Ernest Kador Jr.], American R&B singer ("Mother-in-Law"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2001)
J. Michael Bishop, American immunologist & microbiologist (Nobel Prize 1989), born in York, Pennsylvania
Joanna Russ, American sci-fi author (Hugo, Female Man, Alyx), born in New York City
Chet Walker, American Basketball HOF forward (7 x NBA All-Star; NBA C'ship 1967 Philadelphia 76ers; Chicago Bulls), born in Bethlehem, Mississippi (d. 2024)
Apple's new campus is named Apple Park in Culpertino, California, nicknamed 'the spaceship', designed with Foster + Partners, the first of 12,000 employees to move in, in April
US Women soccer players settle their lawsuit with US soccer federation for $24 million and a promise to equalise pay
Influential French dance duo Daft Punk announce their split after 28 years
Philadelphia centerWilt Chamberlainsets NBA record with 34 free-throw attempts (converts 19) in Warriors 139-121 win over St. Louis Hawks at Philadelphia Civic Center
Sling-shot goal post & 6' wide border around field are standard in NFL
Paul Creston's 1st Symphony premieres
Great White Fleet, 1st US fleet to circle the globe, returns to Virginia
André Malrauxand Édouard Corniglion-Molinier set out to find the lost capital of the Queen of Sheba, as mentioned in the Old Testament
On a bet Edward Weston leaves Boston to walk to Lincoln's inauguration
Tennessee adopts a new constitution abolishing slavery
Peter Arctedi, Swedish biologist ("father of ichthyology" study of fish), born in Anundsjö, Sweden (d. 1735)
